figures.caption: Figure 2: Application of (left) QTP and (right) PTQ. Blue arrows indicate the application of P and PT , which is local to each element (and hence to each processor), while red arrows indicate the application of Q and QT , which require inter-processor communication in cases where adjacent elements are not on the same processor. At the end, one has y = PTQw = PTQQTPx. Q and P are copy operations, while QT and PT involve summation. Global vertex numbers are indicated near the v = 4 vertices of each element.
